# Terraform Azure MySQL Management Module

 [![Latest Release](https://img.shields.io/github/release/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-azurerm-mysql-management.svg?style=for-the-badge)](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-azurerm-mysql-management/releases/latest) [![Last Updated](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-azurerm-mysql-management.svg?style=for-the-badge)](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-azurerm-mysql-management/commits)


Terraform module for managing MySQL databases, users, and grants on an existing Azure MySQL
Flexible Server. Uses the `winebarrel/mysql` provider for database-level management and
stores all generated credentials as JSON secrets in Azure Key Vault. Supports three
connection modes: direct Azure Flexible Server lookup, Key Vault secret (JSON bundle),
and Hoop tunnel (enterprise or community). Hoop enterprise mode additionally stores
per-field connection secrets in Key Vault and produces a structured `hoop_connections`
output consumed by upstream Hoop provider modules.


---

This project is part of our comprehensive approach towards DevOps Acceleration.

It's 100% Open Source and licensed under the [APACHE2](LICENSE).







We have [*lots of terraform modules*][terraform_modules] that are Open Source and we are trying to get them well-maintained!. Check them out!






## Introduction

This module targets teams that need a fully declarative way to bootstrap MySQL databases
and service accounts on Azure MySQL Flexible Server as part of a Terragrunt stack.

Key capabilities:
- Creates one or more MySQL databases with configurable charset and collation.
- Creates `owner` (ALL), `readwrite` (SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE), and `readonly` (SELECT)
  users, each with a randomly generated 24-character password stored in Azure Key Vault.
- Supports password rotation via `password_rotation_period` keeper and `force_reset` flag.
- Three connection modes:
    - **azure** — resolves the Flexible Server FQDN via the AzureRM data source, or reads
      admin credentials from a pre-existing Key Vault secret (JSON bundle).
    - **hoop** — routes through a local Hoop tunnel (community or enterprise).
    - **direct** — plain host/port/username/password for non-Azure or testing scenarios.
- Enterprise Hoop mode writes per-field Key Vault secrets (host, port, user, pass, db) and
  emits a `hoop_connections` output ready for the Hoop Terraform provider.

## Usage


**IMPORTANT:** The `master` branch is used in `source` just as an example. In your code, do not pin to `master` because there may be breaking changes between releases.
Instead pin to the release tag (e.g. `?ref=vX.Y.Z`) of one of our [latest releases](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-azurerm-mysql-management/releases).


```hcl
# terragrunt.hcl
terraform {
  source = "git::https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-azurerm-mysql-management.git//?ref=v1.0.0"
}

inputs = {
  # ── Org / environment ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  org = {
    organization_name = "acme"
    organization_unit = "platform"
    environment_name  = "prod"
    environment_type  = "production"
  }
  region   = "eastus2"
  spoke_def = "01"

  # ── Key Vault ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  key_vault_name                = "kv-platform-prod"       # (Required) Key Vault name
  key_vault_resource_group_name = "rg-platform-prod"       # (Required) Resource group

  # ── Connection mode: Azure Flexible Server (direct lookup) ───────────────────
  azure = {
    enabled              = true              # (Optional) Default: false
    from_secret          = false             # (Optional) Default: false
    server_name          = "mysql-prod-01"   # (Required when enabled+!from_secret)
    resource_group_name  = "rg-mysql-prod"   # (Optional)
    admin_username       = "mysqladmin"       # (Optional) Default: "adminuser"
    admin_password       = "S3cr3t!"         # (Optional)
    db_name              = "mysql"            # (Optional) Default: "mysql"
    sslmode              = "true"             # (Optional) Default: "true"
  }

  # ── Connection mode: credentials from Key Vault secret (JSON bundle) ─────────
  # azure = {
  #   enabled     = true
  #   from_secret = true
  #   secret_name = "mysql-admin-creds"      # (Required when from_secret=true)
  # }

  # ── Connection mode: Hoop tunnel ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  # hoop = {
  #   enabled        = true                  # (Optional) Default: false
  #   community      = false                 # (Optional) Default: true (enterprise=false)
  #   agent_id       = "xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx"  # (Required for enterprise)
  #   port           = 3307                  # (Optional) Default: 3307
  #   db_name        = "mysql"               # (Optional) Default: "mysql"
  #   server_name    = "mysql-prod-01"       # (Optional)
  #   import         = false                 # (Optional) Default: false
  #   tags           = { env = "prod" }      # (Optional)
  #   access_control = ["group-dba"]         # (Optional)
  # }

  # ── Connection mode: direct ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # direct = {
  #   host        = "10.0.0.5"              # (Required)
  #   port        = 3306                    # (Optional) Default: 3306
  #   username    = "admin"                 # (Required)
  #   password    = "S3cr3t!"              # (Required)
  #   db_name     = "mysql"                 # (Optional) Default: "mysql"
  #   server_name = "mysql-prod-01"         # (Optional)
  #   tls         = "true"                  # (Optional) Default: "true"
  # }

  # ── Databases ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  databases = {
    appdb = {
      name      = "appdb"                   # (Required) Actual database name
      charset   = "utf8mb4"                 # (Optional) Default: "utf8mb4"
      collation = "utf8mb4_unicode_ci"      # (Optional) Default: "utf8mb4_unicode_ci"
    }
    reportdb = {
      name = "reportdb"
    }
  }

  # ── Users ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  users = {
    app_owner = {
      username  = "appowner"                # (Required) MySQL user name
      host      = "%"                       # (Optional) Default: "%"
      databases = ["appdb"]                 # (Required) List of databases
      access    = "owner"                   # (Optional) owner/readwrite/readonly. Default: "owner"
    }
    app_reader = {
      username  = "appreader"
      databases = ["appdb", "reportdb"]
      access    = "readonly"
    }
    report_writer = {
      username  = "reportwriter"
      databases = ["reportdb"]
      access    = "readwrite"
    }
  }

  # ── Password management ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  password_rotation_period = "30d"          # (Optional) Default: ""
  force_reset              = false          # (Optional) Default: false
}
```

## Quick Start

1. Ensure the Azure MySQL Flexible Server exists and is reachable.
2. Ensure the Azure Key Vault exists and the Terraform principal has `Key Vault Secrets Officer` role.
3. Reference this module in your Terragrunt stack (see **Usage** above).
4. Run `terragrunt plan` to verify resources, then `terragrunt apply`.
5. Retrieve generated credentials from Key Vault secrets named
   `<prefix>-mysql-<username>-creds` (JSON bundle with `host`, `port`, `username`,
   `password`, `databases`, `server_name`).


## Examples

### Minimal — Azure Flexible Server with owner user

```hcl
inputs = {
  org = { organization_name = "acme", organization_unit = "eng",
          environment_name = "dev", environment_type = "development" }
  region    = "eastus"
  spoke_def = "01"

  key_vault_name                = "kv-eng-dev"
  key_vault_resource_group_name = "rg-eng-dev"

  azure = {
    enabled        = true
    server_name    = "mysql-dev-01"
    admin_username = "mysqladmin"
    admin_password = "DevPass123!"
  }

  databases = { myapp = { name = "myapp" } }
  users = {
    myapp_owner = { username = "myappowner", databases = ["myapp"], access = "owner" }
  }
}
```

## Inputs

| Name | Description | Type | Default | Required |
|------|-------------|------|---------|:--------:|
| <a name="input_azure"></a> [azure](#input\_azure) | Azure MySQL Flexible Server connection settings. Mutually exclusive with direct/hoop. | `any` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_databases"></a> [databases](#input\_databases) | Map of MySQL databases to create. | `any` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_direct"></a> [direct](#input\_direct) | Direct MySQL connection settings. Used when azure.enabled=false and hoop.enabled=false. | `any` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_extra_tags"></a> [extra\_tags](#input\_extra\_tags) | Extra tags to add to the resources | `map(string)` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_force_reset"></a> [force\_reset](#input\_force\_reset) | (Optional) Force password reset for all managed users on next apply. Default: false. | `bool` | `false` | no |
| <a name="input_hoop"></a> [hoop](#input\_hoop) | Hoop connection configuration. Enterprise mode stores per-field secrets in Key Vault. | `any` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_is_hub"></a> [is\_hub](#input\_is\_hub) | Is this a hub or spoke configuration? | `bool` | `false` | no |
| <a name="input_key_vault_name"></a> [key\_vault\_name](#input\_key\_vault\_name) | (Required) Name of the existing Azure Key Vault for credential and hoop secret storage. | `string` | n/a | yes |
| <a name="input_key_vault_resource_group_name"></a> [key\_vault\_resource\_group\_name](#input\_key\_vault\_resource\_group\_name) | (Required) Resource group name of the existing Azure Key Vault. | `string` | n/a | yes |
| <a name="input_org"></a> [org](#input\_org) | Organization details | <pre>object({<br/>    organization_name = string<br/>    organization_unit = string<br/>    environment_type  = string<br/>    environment_name  = string<br/>  })</pre> | n/a | yes |
| <a name="input_password_rotation_period"></a> [password\_rotation\_period](#input\_password\_rotation\_period) | (Optional) Password rotation period for user credentials (e.g., '30d'). Default: empty. | `string` | `""` | no |
| <a name="input_region"></a> [region](#input\_region) | Azure Region to deploy resources into. Example: 'eastus2', defaults to empty string as some of the resources may not require region setting. | `string` | `""` | no |
| <a name="input_spoke_def"></a> [spoke\_def](#input\_spoke\_def) | Spoke ID Number, must be a 3 digit number | `string` | `"001"` | no |
| <a name="input_users"></a> [users](#input\_users) | Map of MySQL users to create with their database assignments. | `any` | `{}` | no |

## Outputs

| Name | Description |
|------|-------------|
| <a name="output_databases"></a> [databases](#output\_databases) | Map of managed MySQL databases. |
| <a name="output_hoop_connections"></a> [hoop\_connections](#output\_hoop\_connections) | Hoop connection definitions for Azure MySQL. Enterprise mode only (Key Vault has no sub-key access). Community mode returns null. |
| <a name="output_users"></a> [users](#output\_users) | Map of managed MySQL users with their Key Vault credential secret names. |
